# Proposal A

## Overview

Pragmatic, flat-layered FastAPI + SQLAlchemy backend paired with a feature-folder Vue 3.5 SPA. Backend is one router per resource (health/auth/cards/tags/covers), one service module per concern (cards/covers/auth/markdown), thin Pydantic schemas, AsyncSession injected via FastAPI Depends. No DDD layers, no repositories — services own transactions and call theORM directly. Frontend is view-as-feature (public views flat, admin views nested under views/admin/), three Pinia setup stores (auth/cards/ui), one openapi-fetch client with a tiny middleware for auth + error normalization, and md-editor-v3/highlight.js as the only heavy dependencies. JWT (HS256, 12h) goes through Authorization: Bearer; auth state persists via pinia-plugin-persistedstate. All endpoints under /api/v1; legacy /api/* paths return a one-release308 shim. Markdown is rendered + sanitized server-side and trusted via v-html on the client. Covers upload to backend/static/covers/ and are served directly by nginx.

```
(see backend tree below + frontend tree under §Frontend)
```

### Modules and responsibilities

| module | responsibility | depends_on |
| --- | --- | --- |
| app/main.py | FastAPI factory create_app(); installs lifespan (engine dispose on shutdown), CORSMiddleware (only when APP_ENV=='dev'), registers exception handlers from errors.py, mounts api_v1_router under /api, registers a catch-all /api/{path:path} legacy shim that308-redirects to /api/v1/{path}, mounts no static (nginx serves covers). | app/settings.py, app/db.py, app/errors.py, app/routers/__init__.py |
| app/settings.py | Settings(BaseSettings) singleton: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, JWT_ALG='HS256', JWT_TTL_SECONDS=43200, LG_ADMIN_USERNAME, LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD, COVERS_DIR (Path), COVERS_PUBLIC_PREFIX='/covers', ALLOWED_ORIGINS (list), APP_ENV('dev'\|'prod'). Reads .env at import time. ||
| app/db.py | Builds AsyncEngine from settings.DATABASE_URL, async_sessionmaker(expire_on_commit=False); registers a sqlalchemy event'connect' listener that runs PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 when dialect is sqlite; exposes get_db() async generator that yields a session, rollbacks on exception, closes in finally. | app/settings.py |
| app/models.py | Base(DeclarativeBase) with MetaData(naming_convention={'ix':'ix_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_N_name)s','uq':'uq_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_N_name)s','ck':'ck_%(table_name)s_%(constraint_name)s','fk':'fk_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_N_name)s_%(referred_table_name)s','pk':'pk_%(table_name)s'}); TimestampMixin (created_at, updated_at). Defines User and Card with typed Mapped[]. |  |
| app/deps.py | Type aliases for FastAPI dependencies: DbDep = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)]; CurrentUserDep = Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)] which decodes Authorization header, loads user from DB, raises LGException('UNAUTHENTICATED'); AdminUserDep = Annotated[User, Depends(require_admin)] which extends CurrentUserDep and asserts role=='admin', raises LGException('FORBIDDEN'). | app/db.py, app/models.py, app/security.py, app/errors.py, app/settings.py |
| app/errors.py | LGException(code: str, message: str, status: int = 400) raised throughout the app. Three FastAPI exception handlers: (1) LGException -> JSONResponse({'ok':False,'error':msg,'code':code}, status); (2) RequestValidationError -> {'ok':False,'error':'invalid request body','code':'INVALID_BODY','detail':errs},422; (3) StarletteHTTPException -> envelope using a code derived from status (404→NOT_FOUND, etc). |  |
| app/security.py | hash_password(pw)/verify_password(pw, hashed) wrapping bcrypt directly (truncates to 72 bytes, raises LGException if longer). encode_token(user) returns JWT with claims {sub:user_id_str, username, role, iat, exp} using settings.JWT_SECRET + HS256. decode_token(token) verifies signature with algorithms=['HS256'], returns claims dict or raises LGException('UNAUTHENTICATED'). | app/settings.py, app/errors.py |
| app/slug.py | slugify(text) -> str (lowercase; replace whitespace with '-'; keep [a-z0-9-一-鿿]; strip leading/trailing '-'; fallback to 'article-<short_uuid>' when empty). unique_slug(session, base, exclude_id=None) -> str: queries Card rows with archived=False, finds the lowest -2/-3/... suffix that is unused (the partial unique index is the safety net). | app/models.py |
| app/routers/health.py | GET /health -> {'ok': True}. Mirrored mount also exists at bare /api/health (registered directly in main.py) so external monitors stay on a stable path. |  |
| app/routers/auth.py | POST /auth/login (body LoginRequest) -> TokenResponse via services.auth.authenticate + mint_token; GET /auth/me (CurrentUserDep) -> UserPublic. | app/deps.py, app/services/auth.py, app/schemas/auth.py, app/errors.py |
| app/routers/cards.py | All6 card endpoints. Reads CardListQuery via Query(...) bundle; calls services/cards. Requires AdminUserDep on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE; GET endpoints are public. | app/deps.py, app/services/cards.py, app/schemas/cards.py, app/errors.py |
| app/routers/tags.py | GET /tags?include_archived= -> list[str] via services.cards.list_tags; default excludes archived (matches /cards default). | app/deps.py, app/services/cards.py |
| app/routers/covers.py | POST /covers (multipart: file: UploadFile, card_id: UUID = Form). Verifies card exists, calls services.covers.save_upload, returns CoverUploadResponse. Updates Card.cover to the returned public URL atomically. | app/deps.py, app/services/covers.py, app/services/cards.py, app/schemas/covers.py, app/errors.py |
| app/services/markdown.py | render_markdown(md: str) -> str. Configures markdown-it-py with linkify=True, html=False, plugins: footnote, anchors disabled (no slugged headings), table, deflist; emits fenced_code with class='language-X' and data-language='X' attribute on <code>. Pipes output through nh3.clean(html, tags={'h1','h2','h3','h4','h5','h6','p','ul','ol','li','blockquote','pre','code','a','img','table','thead','tbody','tr','th','td','hr','em','strong','br','span','div'}, attributes={'a':{'href','title','rel','target'},'img':{'src','alt','title'},'code':{'class','data-language'},'span':{'class'},'div':{'class'}}, url_schemes={'http','https','mailto'}); strips a leading '# H1' line before render (preserves today's behavior). |  |
| app/services/cards.py | list_cards(session, filters: CardListQuery) -> list[Card] with WHERE archived=False unless include_archived; case-insensitive q filter on title \|\| summary \|\| tags JSON; ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC. get_card_by_slug(session, slug) (raises LGException('CARD_NOT_FOUND',404) if missing). create_card(session, payload, author_id): mints uuid4(), runs slugify+unique_slug, validates category↔(url\|body), renders body_html lazily (no — render on read; see notes). update_card(session, card_id, payload): partial update, fields omitted are kept (fixes today's PUT bug); never changes slug. set_archive(session, card_id, archived). delete_card(session, card_id): unlinks cover file if cover starts with COVERS_PUBLIC_PREFIX. list_tags(session, include_archived) -> sorted distinct flatten of cards.tags. Markdown rendering happens in get_card_by_slug only (cached via @lru_cache keyed on (id, updated_at)). | app/models.py, app/slug.py, app/services/markdown.py, app/errors.py, app/settings.py |
| app/services/covers.py | save_upload(file: UploadFile, card_id: UUID) -> str. Validates content-type ∈ {image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp}; reads bytes (max 5 MiB; raises COVER_TOO_LARGE); opens with Pillow, verifies dims≤ 4096×4096; picks ext from MIME; writes atomically (tmp + os.replace) to settings.COVERS_DIR/<card_id>.<ext>; returns f'{settings.COVERS_PUBLIC_PREFIX}/{card_id}.{ext}'. Old extension files for the same card_id (otherext) get unlinked. | app/settings.py, app/errors.py |
| app/services/auth.py | authenticate(session, username, password) -> User or raise LGException('INVALID_CREDENTIALS',401); seed_admin(session, settings) called from alembic 0001 (op.run_async-equivalent via op.bulk_insert with pre-hashed password) and from a test fixture; mint_token(user) wraps security.encode_token. | app/models.py, app/security.py, app/settings.py, app/errors.py |


#### cards

Columns:
```
id: Mapped[UUID] = mapped_column(primary_key=True, default=uuid4); slug: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False); title: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False); category: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(16), nullable=False)# 'external' | 'local'; group: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(16), nullable=True)  # '技术类' | '随笔类' | '生活类'; summary: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False, default=''); cover: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(512), nullable=True); url: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(2048), nullable=True); body: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True); tags: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False, default=list); archived: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, nullable=False, default=False); author_id: Mapped[UUID | None] = mapped_column(ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL'), nullable=True); created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False); updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now(), nullable=False)
```

Indexes:
- ix_cards_slug_active UNIQUE (slug) WHERE archived = false-- partial unique; slug uniqueness only among non-archived (works on SQLite >= 3.8and PostgreSQL)
- ix_cards_archived_created_at (archived, created_at DESC)
- ix_cards_category (category)
- ix_cards_group (group)
- ix_cards_author_id (author_id)
- ck_cards_category CHECK (category IN ('external','local'))

Relationships:
- author: Mapped[User | None] = relationship(back_populates='cards')


### Pydantic schemas

| name | fields | used_by |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ErrorResponse | ok: Literal[False] = False; error: str; code: str; detail: list[dict] \| None = None | all error responses (declared as the 4xx response_model in router signatures via responses={400: {'model': ErrorResponse}, ...}) |
| OkResponse | ok: Literal[True] = True | DELETE /cards/{id}, COVER on success-only, generic acks |
| LoginRequest | username: str (min 1, max 64); password: str (min 1, max 256) | POST /auth/login body |
| UserPublic | id: UUID; username: str; role: str; created_at: datetime; model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) | GET /auth/me, embedded in TokenResponse |
| TokenResponse | access_token: str; token_type: Literal['bearer'] = 'bearer'; expires_in: int; user: UserPublic | POST /auth/login response |
| CardCategory | Literal['external', 'local']# type alias | Card schemas |
| CardGroup | Literal['技术类', '随笔类', '生活类']  # extensible — keep as Literal until DB migration adds enum table | Card schemas |
| CardListQuery | category: CardCategory \| None = None; group: CardGroup \| None = None; tag: str \| None = None; q: str \| None = None; include_archived: bool = False# bound via Query(...) in router signature | GET /cards |
| CardCreate | title: str (min 1, max 255); category: CardCategory; group: CardGroup \| None = None; summary: str = ''; cover: str \| None = None; tags: list[str] = []# validated: trim, dedupe ci, max 16 tags, each≤32 chars; url: str \| None = None  # required iff category=='external', validated as http(s) URL; body: str \| None = None  # required iff category=='local'; slug_seed: str \| None = None  # optional override of slug source; @model_validator enforces category↔(url\|body) coupling | POST /cards body |
| CardUpdate | title: str \| None = None; category: CardCategory \| None = None; group: CardGroup \| None = None; summary: str \| None = None; cover: str \| None = None; tags: list[str] \| None = None; url: str \| None = None; body: str \| None = None; archived: bool \| None = None  # all optional; only fields present (model_dump(exclude_unset=True)) get applied — fixes the legacy 'PUT silently wipes summary/cover' bug | PUT /cards/{id} body |
| ArchiveRequest | archived: bool  # required; no default — fixes legacy 'empty body archives' surprise | PATCH /cards/{id}/archive body |
| CardPublic | id: UUID; slug: str; title: str; category: CardCategory; group: CardGroup \| None; summary: str; cover: str \| None; url: str \| None; tags: list[str]; archived: bool; created_at: datetime; updated_at: datetime; model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) | GET /cards items, POST /cards / PUT /cards / PATCH archive responses |
| CardDetail | extends CardPublic with: body: str \| None = None# raw md, H1-stripped; body_html: str \| None = None  # sanitized html — both populated only when category=='local' | GET /cards/{slug} response |
| CoverUploadResponse |ok: Literal[True] = True; url: str  # e.g. /covers/<uuid>.png; card: CardPublic  # the updated card with new cover field | POST /covers response |

### API v1 contract

| method | path | auth | request | response_2xx | response_4xx | notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GET | /api/health | public | — | {ok: true} | — | Stable monitor path; mounted directly in main.py outside the /v1 prefix so external monitoring never breaks on a version bump. |
| GET | /api/v1/health | public | — | {ok: true} | — | Mirror under /v1 for consistency. |
| POST | /api/v1/auth/login | public | JSON LoginRequest | TokenResponse {access_token, token_type:'bearer', expires_in:43200, user:UserPublic} | 401ErrorResponse{code:'INVALID_CREDENTIALS'}; 422 ErrorResponse{code:'INVALID_BODY'} | Constant-time bcrypt compare; identical 401 message for missing user vs bad password. |
| GET | /api/v1/auth/me | bearer-any | — | UserPublic | 401 ErrorResponse{code:'UNAUTHENTICATED'} | Used by frontend on app boot to validate persisted token. |
| GET | /api/v1/cards | public | Query CardListQuery: category?, group?, tag?, q?, include_archived? | list[CardPublic] | 422 ErrorResponse{code:'INVALID_BODY'} | Default excludes archived. Sort: created_at DESC, id DESC. q is case-insensitive substring across title \|\| summary \|\| tags. tag is exact-match (case-insensitive) against any element of cards.tags. |
| GET | /api/v1/cards/{slug} | public | Path slug:str | CardDetail (body+body_html only for category=='local') | 404 ErrorResponse{code:'CARD_NOT_FOUND'} | body_html is server-rendered + sanitized via services/markdown.py. Looks up by slug, NOT id. Cached at the service layer keyed on (card.id, updated_at). |
| POST | /api/v1/cards | bearer-admin | JSON CardCreate | 201 CardPublic | 400 ErrorResponse{code:'MISSING_FIELD'\|'INVALID_CATEGORY'}; 422 INVALID_BODY; 401/403 | Mints UUID id, computes slug from slug_seed\|\|title, regenerates with -2/-3 on collision among non-archived. external⇒url required; local⇒body required. |
| PUT | /api/v1/cards/{id} | bearer-admin | Path id:UUID; JSON CardUpdate (partial) | CardPublic | 404 CARD_NOT_FOUND; 400 INVALID_CATEGORY; 422 INVALID_BODY; 401/403 | Only present fields applied (model_dump(exclude_unset=True)). Slug NEVER changes. Switching category enforces required url/body coupling on the resulting state. |
| PATCH | /api/v1/cards/{id}/archive | bearer-admin | Path id:UUID; JSON ArchiveRequest | CardPublic | 404 CARD_NOT_FOUND; 422 INVALID_BODY; 401/403 | Setter (not toggle); body required. Archiving a card with a slug that has a non-archived twin succeeds because partial unique index permits it once archived=true. |
| DELETE | /api/v1/cards/{id} | bearer-admin | Path id:UUID | OkResponse | 404 CARD_NOT_FOUND; 401/403 | Hard delete. Unlinks cover file under settings.COVERS_DIR if cover URL starts with settings.COVERS_PUBLIC_PREFIX. UI keeps the button hidden per PROJECT_NOTES; endpoint exists for ops use. |
| GET | /api/v1/tags | public | Query include_archived?: bool | list[str] (sorted, distinct) | — | Default excludes archived (fixes today's bug where tags leak from archived cards while /cards excludes them). |
| POST | /api/v1/covers | bearer-admin | multipart/form-data: file: UploadFile, card_id: UUID | CoverUploadResponse {ok, url, card} | 400 BAD_REQUEST; 404 CARD_NOT_FOUND; 413 COVER_TOO_LARGE; 415 COVER_BAD_TYPE; 401/403 | MIME ∈ {image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp}; ≤ 5 MiB; ≤ 4096×4096 verified via Pillow magic bytes (defense vs MIME spoof). Atomic write tmp + os.replace. Updates card.cover and returns the updated card. |
| ANY | /api/{path:path}  (legacy shim — non-/v1) | passthrough | any | 308 Permanent Redirect to /api/v1/{path} | — | Registered with low priority in main.py; only catches paths not starting with v1/ or health. 308 preserves method+body. Removed one release after the new frontend ships. |

### Auth flow

**Login Flow:** Frontend POSTs JSON {username, password} to /api/v1/auth/login. services.auth.authenticate(session, u, p) selects the user by username, runs bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode()[:72], user.password_hash); on mismatch raises LGException('INVALID_CREDENTIALS',401). On success, mint_token(user) returns the JWT and the handler returns TokenResponse. Frontend stores access_token + user in the auth Pinia store (persisted via pinia-plugin-persistedstate to localStorage) and starts including'Authorization: Bearer <token>' on every subsequent request via the openapi-fetch middleware.
**Password Hash:** bcrypt 4.x called directly (no passlib). hash_password(pw) -> bcrypt.hashpw(pw.encode()[:72], bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12)).decode(). verify_password(pw, hashed) -> bcrypt.checkpw(pw.encode()[:72], hashed.encode()). 72-byte truncation documented in README. Reject passwords shorter than 8 chars at the schema layer (LoginRequest min_length=1for login but the seed/create_admin script enforces ≥8).
**Jwt Claims:** Algorithm HS256 only (decode pinned). Claims: {sub: str(user.id), username: str, role: 'admin'|..., iat: int, exp: int}. Secret from settings.JWT_SECRET (≥32 bytes; generated via `openssl rand -hex 32`).
**Token Lifetime:** 12 hours (43200 seconds) via settings.JWT_TTL_SECONDS. Single-tenant single-admin app — long-lived token is acceptable. expires_in returned alongside access_token so the frontend can preempt refresh-by-relogin a few minutes early if desired.
**Refresh Strategy:** No refresh token in v1. On any 401 from the API, the openapi-fetch middleware clears the auth store and (if the current route requires admin) redirects to /admin/login with a ?next= return URL. Re-login is the refresh mechanism. Refresh tokens deferred until multi-user/role expansion.
**Guard Decorator:** FastAPI Depends, no custom decorator. Type aliases in app/deps.py: CurrentUserDep = Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)] decodes the Authorization header (raises UNAUTHENTICATED on missing/invalid/expired token), loads the user row, returns it. AdminUserDep = Annotated[User, Depends(require_admin)] composes CurrentUserDep and asserts role=='admin' (raises FORBIDDEN otherwise). Routes write `_: AdminUserDep` as a parameter to enforce.

### Error envelope and exception handlers

- Error envelope is exactly {ok: false, error: string, code: string, detail?: any}; success bodies are the resource itself (CardPublic / CardDetail / list / TokenResponse) — no top-level {ok: true, data: ...} wrapping for resource reads. — Resource shape parity with FastAPI's natural OpenAPI types; codegen produces clean components/schemas. Errors are the only place the envelope shows up, which keeps the success path1:1 with the OpenAPI types and trivially typed via openapi-fetch.

- Stable error codes: INVALID_BODY, MISSING_FIELD, INVALID_CATEGORY, CARD_NOT_FOUND, SLUG_CONFLICT, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, COVER_TOO_LARGE, COVER_BAD_TYPE, COVER_DIM_TOO_LARGE, INTERNAL. — Frontend can branch on code without parsing free-text error strings; codes never localized.


## Migration plan

backend/scripts/migrate_from_json.py — one-shot, idempotent CLI script.

USAGE: `uv run python -m scripts.migrate_from_json --json-file ../data/cards.json --notes-dir ../content/notes --owner-username admin [--dry-run]`

BEHAVIOR (single async transaction per card):
1. Bootstrap: ensure DB schema is current (`alembic upgrade head` is the operator's responsibility and runs before this script). Open AsyncSession.
2. Resolve owner: SELECT users WHERE username=owner_username. If missing, abort with a clear message("seed admin first via alembic 0001 or scripts/create_admin.py").
3. Parse cards.json. For each entry, in legacy file order:
   a. legacy_id = entry['id']. Look up SELECT cards WHERE slug=legacy_id (no archived filter — old data is the snapshot). If found, log "skip (already migrated)" and CONTINUE. This is the idempotency key.
   b. Mint new_id = uuid4().
   c. Map fields: title=entry['title']; category=entry['category']; group=None (legacy lacks it); summary=entry.get('summary',''); cover=entry.get('cover') or None; tags=entry.get('tags') or []; archived=entry.get('archived', False); created_at=parse('YYYY-MM-DD' +'T00:00:00+00:00') from entry['created_at']; updated_at=created_at; author_id=owner.id.
   d. If category=='external': url=entry['url']; body=None.
   e. If category=='local': md_path = notes_dir / Path(entry['markdown']).name; raise if missing; body = md_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'); url=None. Markdown body stored RAW (H1 not stripped — strip happens at render time).
   f. INSERT card row. On UNIQUE-violation (partial slug index), suffix '-imported' and retry once; abort otherwise.
4. After all entries, log summary: inserted=N, skipped=M, errors=K. Exit 0 only if errors=0.
5. --dry-run: prints the same plan without committing (rolls back the outer transaction).

IDEMPOTENCY: keyed solely on slug uniqueness within non-archived. Re-running on a populated DB produces 0 inserts. Operator is told NOT to re-run after edits — to force re-import, manually `DELETE FROM cards WHERE slug='<x>'` first.

ROLLBACK PROCEDURE (manual; documented in docs/refactor/migration-runbook.md):
- For aiosqlite: `mv linkgarden.db linkgarden.db.bak && alembic upgrade head` recreates an empty schema; restore the JSON snapshot to data/cards.json and revert the systemd unit to the legacy Flask deploy. Old data/cards.json + content/notes/ are preserved as a read-only snapshot per the locked decisions.
- For Postgres swap: `pg_restore` the pre-migration dump.

The legacy data/cards.json and content/notes/*.md remain on disk untouched after migration. After a successful migration + verification, the deploy script chmods them to0444 to prevent accidental writes. Orphan note (multi-agent-super-universe-draft.md) is logged but NOT inserted — it lives in /var/log/linkgarden as a one-time WARN.

VERIFICATION (run after migration):
- `pytest tests/test_migrate_from_json.py` exercises the script against a fresh in-memory DB with a fixture cards.json.
- A spot-check command `uv run python -m scripts.spot_check` lists card counts by category and tag distribution; operator compares to the legacy `/api/cards` snapshot before flipping nginx to the new backend.

## Deployment plan

SINGLE HOST. Two systemd units (backend gunicorn + nothing for frontend; nginx serves the static dist directly). pnpm for frontend, uv for backend.

LAYOUT ON HOST:
/srv/projects/link-garden/
├── backend/          # git checkout; .venv lives here (uv-managed)
│   ├── .env# secrets (chmod 600, owner linkgarden)
│   ├── linkgarden.db (or pg connection)
│   └── static/covers/
└── frontend/         # plain rsynced dist; no node_modules in prod

deploy/systemd/linkgarden-backend.service:
[Unit]
Description=Link Garden FastAPI backend
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=notify
User=linkgarden
Group=linkgarden
WorkingDirectory=/srv/projects/link-garden/backend
EnvironmentFile=/srv/projects/link-garden/backend/.env
ExecStartPre=/srv/projects/link-garden/backend/.venv/bin/alembic upgrade head
ExecStart=/srv/projects/link-garden/backend/.venv/bin/gunicorn app.main:app \\
  -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker \\
  -w 2 \\
  --bind 127.0.0.1:5001 \\
  --proxy-headers --forwarded-allow-ips=127.0.0.1 \\
  --access-logfile - --error-logfile -
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ReadWritePaths=/srv/projects/link-garden/backend/static /srv/projects/link-garden/backend/linkgarden.db

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

NOTES on the unit: workers=2 is the right starting point (UvicornWorker is async; SQLite serializes writes anyway).ExecStartPre runs alembic upgrade head every restart — Alembic is no-op on idempotent revisions. systemd's Type=notify works with uvicorn workers via gunicorn's notify support.

deploy/nginx/linkgarden.conf (sketch):
server {
  listen 443 ssl http2;
  server_name linkgarden.example.com;
  ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/.../fullchain.pem;
  ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/.../privkey.pem;

  root /srv/projects/link-garden/frontend;
  index index.html;
  client_max_body_size 6m;  # > 5MB cover limit + slop

  location / { try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html; }

  location /api/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5001;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;

  }

  location /covers/ {

  }
}

DEPLOY STEPS (script in scripts/deploy.sh):
1. Backend: `git pull && cd backend && uv sync --no-dev` on host. systemctl restart linkgarden-backend (ExecStartPre runs alembic upgrade head before gunicorn binds).
2. Frontend: locally `cd frontend && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm gen:api && pnpm build`. Then `rsync -avz --delete frontend/dist/ user@host:/srv/projects/link-garden/frontend/`. nginx auto-picks up new files; no reload needed.
3. systemctl reload nginx is required only when deploy/nginx/linkgarden.conf itself changes.
4. Health check: `curl -fsS https://linkgarden.example.com/api/health` after step 1; `curl -fsS https://linkgarden.example.com/` after step 2.
5. Cutover from legacy: rename current legacy systemd unit (linkgarden.service) to linkgarden-legacy.service.disabled, then enable+start linkgarden-backend.service. Roll back is a swap of those two unit names + restoring data/cards.json from snapshot.

ALEMBIC AS DEPLOY STEP: `alembic upgrade head` runs inExecStartPre with the same .env that gunicorn uses. Alembic 0001 ships an op.bulk_insert that seeds the admin user from LG_ADMIN_USERNAME / LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD when no users exist (uses bcrypt-hashed password computed in the migration script — settings imported inside the upgrade() function, never at module level).

CI (.github/workflows; deferred but planned): on push, run `uv run ruff check`, `uv run pyright`, `uv run pytest`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm gen:api && git diff --exit-code openapi/schema.json src/api/schema.d.ts`.

## Risks and mitigations

| risk | mitigation |
| --- | --- |
| SQLite single-writer bottleneck under concurrent admin writes. | WAL + busy_timeout=5000; gunicorn workers ≤ 2; admin workload is single-tenant. Postgres swap is one DATABASE_URL change away (asyncpg already declared as optional dep). |
| Alembic env.py async setup lands wrong (#1 cause of'alembic upgrade head' hangs). | Use the cookbook async pattern: connection.run_sync(do_migrations) with render_as_batch=True for sqlite; explicitly `from app.models import *` in env.py to populate target_metadata. |
| Pydantic v2 / SQLAlchemy 2 typed Mapped[] migration produces silent type errors that pyright flags but humans miss. | pyright in strict mode on app/, basic on tests/, gated in pre-commit; commits blocked until clean. |
| JWT secret leak via accidental commit or env dump. | .env in .gitignore; .env.example carries placeholders only; secret read by pydantic-settings at import time so a missing/short secret crashes uvicorn loudly before the first request. |
| nh3 strips legitimate markdown output (e.g. footnote refs, custom span classes). | Allowlist explicitly enumerated in services/markdown.py with tests covering: headings, inline code, fenced code with language, tables, blockquote, links (http/https/mailto only), images (http/https), strong/em, footnotes via mdit-plugin. Tests pin the expected sanitized output. |
| openapi-typescript codegen drifts vs server, frontend types lie. | CI step: `pnpm gen:api && git diff --exit-code openapi/schema.json src/api/schema.d.ts` fails the pipeline if not regenerated; openapi/schema.json and src/api/schema.d.ts both committed. |
| Legacy /api/*308 shim breaks clients that don't preserve body across redirects (older curl, some fetch polyfills). | Frontend cuts over to /api/v1 in the same release that adds the shim; shim is for the brief window where the live frontend still hits /api/*. Documented removal date in CHANGELOG. |
| Cover upload abuse — large files, MIME spoof, decompression bombs. | 5 MiB hard cap (read-stream + early abort); MIME from Content-Type + magic-byte verify via Pillow.open(BytesIO(payload)).verify(); dimension cap 4096×4096; only image/{png,jpeg,webp}; admin-only endpoint behind JWT. |
| Markdown rendering on every /cards/{slug} request burns CPU. | @lru_cache(maxsize=128) keyed on (card.id, updated_at) inside services/cards.get_card_by_slug; cache invalidates naturally on update because updated_at flips. |
| Partial unique index on slug is not portable to all SQLite < 3.8. | pyproject pins python>=3.12and aiosqlite which bundles modern SQLite (3.40+); deploy host check enforces sqlite3 ≥ 3.8(covered byUbuntu 22.04+ default). |
| Pinia 3 setup stores have no auto $reset, easy to leak state on logout. | Each store explicitly defines a $reset action that nulls/resets everyref it returns; auth.logout() calls it; tests assert post-logout state. |
| highlight.js double-bundling (md-editor-v3ships its own copy). | pnpm overrides pin a single highlight.js version; useHighlight.ts registers only the languages we use (ts, js, py, sh, json, html, css, vue, sql, md) keeping the bundle≤50KB. |
| Vite dev server can't reach FastAPI without proxy (today's bug). | vite.config.ts ships server.proxy['/api'] = http://127.0.0.1:5001 from day one; documented in frontend/README. |
| Migration script run twice creates duplicates if slug index missing. | Script wraps each card insert in a savepoint; partial unique index on slug is the safety net; idempotency check is the primary defense (SELECT by slug before insert). |
| WAL-mode SQLite on NFS or networked filesystem corrupts. | Deploy onto local disk (/srv) only; explicit note in deploy/README. Docker volume must be a local bind mount, not an NFS share. |
| OAuth2/JWT token in localStorage is XSS-readable. | Sanitization is server-side (nh3); no third-party scripts loaded; CSP header (set in nginx) restricts script sources to'self'. Long-term move to httpOnly cookies is documented but deferred. |
designB:
# Proposal B

## Overview
Proposal B is a strict layered architecture: HTTP routers stay thin and delegate to a service layer, which in turn calls repositories that own all SQLAlchemy queries. Pydantic schemas are split into Read/Write/Admin variants per resource so the wire contract stays explicit and the OpenAPI document is easy to consume from `openapi-typescript`. The frontend mirrors this with a generated `api/` client, composables for cross-view logic, and a container/presentational view split — views own data fetching and stores, while components stay pure. UUID `id` is the immutable PK; `slug` is the URL handle and is regenerable. Markdown is rendered server-side through markdown-it-py + nh3 with strict allow-listing, and the client trusts the resulting HTML. The whole thing ships as a monorepo with one systemd unit, alembic-driven migrations as an `ExecStartPre`, and rsync deploys for the Vite build.

| `app/security/jwt.py` | `create_access_token`, `decode_token` (HS256, pinned) | pyjwt |
| `app/core/errors.py` | Domain exceptions with `code` + `http_status` | — |
| `app/asgi.py` | `app = create_app()` for `gunicorn app.asgi:app` | `app.main` |

### Pydantic schemas

All schemas use `ConfigDict(from_attributes=True, str_strip_whitespace=True)`. Validators enforce: tags are `list[str]`, deduped, max 16 entries, each≤ 32 chars; `url` constrained by `AnyHttpUrl`; `cover` constrained by `AnyHttpUrl | path-starting-with-/covers/`; `group` is a `Literal["技术类","随笔类","生活类"]`.

| Resource | Read | Write | Admin |
|---|---|---|---|
| Card | `CardListItem` (id, slug, title, category, group, summary, tags, cover, created_at, archived), `CardRead` (CardListItem + url, body, body_html), `CardDetailRead` (CardRead minus body when external) | `CardCreate` (category, group, title, summary?, tags?, cover?, slug?, url\|content), `CardUpdate` (all fields Optional, explicit `None` semantics via `model_fields_set`), `CardArchive` (`archived: bool = True`) | `CardAdminRead` adds `updated_at`, raw `body` always |
| User | `UserRead` (id, username, role, created_at) | — (no self-registration in v1) | `AdminUserRead` adds `created_at`; `UserCreate` (username, password) used only by seeder |
| Auth | `TokenResponse` (access_token, token_type='bearer', expires_in) | `LoginRequest` (username, password) | — |
| Tag | `list[str]` (no envelope) | — | — |
| Cover | `CoverUploadResponse` (url, width, height, bytes) | `CoverUploadRequest` is multipart, validated at endpoint | — |
| Common | `ErrorEnvelope` (`ok: Literal[False]`, `error: str`, `code: str`), `OkResponse` (`ok: Literal[True]`) | — | — |

### API v1 contract

All paths prefixed `/api/v1`. Auth: `none` (public), `bearer` (any user), `admin` (role check). Mutating routes require `admin`. The legacy `/api/*` paths return `308Permanent Redirect` to the v1 equivalent for one release.

| Method | Path | Auth | Request | 2xx | 4xx | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GET | `/health` | none | — | `200 {"ok": true}` | — | Liveness; never touches DB |
| POST | `/auth/login` | none | `LoginRequest` | `200 TokenResponse` | `401 invalid_credentials` | bcrypt verify; rate-limited via slowapi (10/min/IP) |
| GET | `/auth/me` | bearer | — | `200 UserRead` | `401 unauthenticated` | Reads current_user dep |
| GET | `/cards` | none | query: `category?`, `group?`, `tag?`, `q?`, `include_archived?` | `200 list[CardListItem]` | `422 invalid_query` | `q` matches title+summary+tags ILIKE; default excludes archived;ordered by `created_at DESC` |
| GET | `/cards/{slug}` | none | — | `200 CardRead` (`body_html` only when local) | `404 card_not_found` | Lookup by `slug`. If archived and caller is not admin, returns 404 |
| POST | `/cards` | admin | `CardCreate` | `201 CardRead` | `400 missing_url \| missing_content`, `409 slug_taken`, `422` | Server mints UUID, derives unique `slug` from explicit `slug` or `title`. Renders + caches `body_html` for local |
| PUT | `/cards/{id}` | admin | `CardUpdate` | `200 CardRead` | `404 card_not_found`, `400 invalid_category_switch`, `422` | Lookup by UUID `id`. Category switch wipes the stale field (url↔ body) and re-renders. `slug` is regenerable: if `slug` provided, validate uniqueness; otherwise leave |
| PATCH | `/cards/{id}/archive` | admin | `CardArchive` | `200 CardRead` | `404 card_not_found` | Setter, body `{archived: bool}`, default `True` preserved for back-compat |
| DELETE | `/cards/{id}` | admin | — | `204` | `404 card_not_found` | Hard delete; deletes orphan cover file |
| GET | `/tags` | none | query: `include_archived?` | `200 list[str]` | — | Distinct sorted; archived excluded by default (fixes P1 bug) |
| POST | `/covers` | admin | multipart: `file`, `card_id` | `201 CoverUploadResponse` | `400 invalid_image`, `404 card_not_found`, `413 file_too_large`, `415 unsupported_media_type` | Validates magic bytes (png/jpeg/webp), max 5 MiB, dims 200..4096, writes `<static>/covers/<card_id>.<ext>`, updates `cards.cover` to public URL |

### Auth flow

1. `POST /auth/login` accepts `{username, password}`. `auth_service.authenticate` calls `user_repo.get_by_username` and `verify_password`. On success it mints `create_access_token({"sub": str(user.id), "role": user.role}, expires=settings.jwt_ttl)`.
2. The token is returned in JSON: `{access_token, token_type:"bearer", expires_in}`. No cookies.
3. Client stores it in a Pinia store (in-memory) plus persisted shadow in `localStorage` (key `lg_auth`). Every request adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
4. `api/deps.py::get_current_user` reads the header, decodes with `algorithms=["HS256"]`, fetches the User by `sub`, raises `Unauthorized("token_invalid")` on any failure.
5. `require_admin` is a dependency that wraps `get_current_user` and asserts `role == "admin"` else `Forbidden("forbidden")`.
6. Initial admin: `alembic` revision `0002_seed_admin` reads `LG_ADMIN_USERNAME` and `LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD` from env at upgrade time, hashes the password, and inserts the row only if no users exist (idempotent). A `scripts/seed_admin.py` is also provided for manual rotation.
7. Token TTL default24h; refresh is out of scope for v1 — re-login is acceptable for a single-admin tool. Logout is purely client-side (drop the token).

### Markdown pipeline

`services/markdown.py` exposes `render(md: str) -> RenderedMarkdown` returning `{html, plain_summary, toc}` (TOC reserved, not surfaced).

1. Strip a leading `# H1` line (preserves PROJECT_NOTES rule).
2. `MarkdownIt("gfm-like", {"linkify": True, "html": False, "typographer": False})` with plugins: `tables`, `footnote`, `fenced_code` (built-in), `task_lists`. `html=False` ensures inline HTML in source is escaped — defense in depth.
3. Post-process the token stream: for every `fence` token, set attribute `data-language=<info>` so the frontend can hand it to `highlight.js`.
4. Sanitize with `nh3.clean(html, tags=ALLOW_TAGS, attributes=ALLOW_ATTRS)` where `ALLOW_TAGS` is an explicit allowlist (`p, h1..h6, ul, ol, li, blockquote, pre, code, em, strong, a, img, table, thead, tbody, tr, th, td, hr, br, span, sup, sub, del, input[type=checkbox]`) and `ALLOW_ATTRS` permits `class` on `code/pre/span`, `data-language` on `pre`, `href`/`title` on `a`, `src`/`alt`/`title`/`loading` on `img`. `a` gets `rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow"` and `target="_blank"` rewritten via `nh3.AttributeFilter`.
5. The result is stored in `cards.body_html` on every write; reads serve the cached HTML directly. A unit test in `tests/unit/test_markdown.py` pins both happy path and the XSS allowlist (script, on*, javascript: href, etc.).
6. Frontend renders via `v-html` and runs a tiny `enhanceCodeBlocks` composable that attaches `highlight.js` per `pre[data-language]` exactly once (idempotent per PROJECT_NOTES).

### Cover upload pipeline

1. Endpoint: `POST /api/v1/covers`, `multipart/form-data` with parts `file` (`UploadFile`) and `card_id` (UUID).
2. `cover_service.upload(file, card_id, session)`:
   - `card_repo.get_by_id(card_id)` → 404 if absent.
   - Reject if `file.size > 5 MiB` (415/413).
   - Sniff first 12 bytes; allow `png`, `jpeg`, `webp` only.
   - `Pillow.Image.open(stream).verify()`, then re-open to read dims; reject if `<200` or `>4096` on either axis.
   - Compute extension from sniffed type (not from `filename`).
   - Atomic write: write to `<static_dir>/covers/<card_id>.<ext>.tmp`, `os.replace` to final path. Old extensions for the same `card_id` are unlinked first.
   - Update `cards.cover = "/static/covers/<card_id>.<ext>?v=<timestamp>"` for cache-busting.
3. Static serving: FastAPI mounts `StaticFiles(directory=settings.static_dir)` at `/static`. In prod nginx serves the directory directly (location `/static/`).
4. Response: `CoverUploadResponse{url, width, height, bytes}`.

### Error envelope and exception handlers

- `core/errors.py` defines `AppError(code: str, message: str, http_status: int)` plus subclasses `NotFound`, `Conflict`, `Unauthorized`, `Forbidden`, `BadRequest`, `Unprocessable`.
- `api/errors.py` registers three handlers on the FastAPI app:
  1. `AppError` → `JSONResponse(status_code=exc.http_status, content={"ok": False, "error": exc.message, "code": exc.code})`.
  2. `HTTPException` → same envelope, `code` derived from `exc.detail` if it is a known machine code, else `"http_<status>"`.
  3. `RequestValidationError` → `code="validation_failed"`, `error` is the first error string; full `errors` array attached under `details` for clients that want it.
- Success responses are bare resources (no envelope) per the locked decision; only `/health` and `OkResponse` returns include `ok: true`.
- All handlers log via `structlog` with `request_id`, `path`, `method`, `code`.

## Migration plan

`scripts/migrate_from_json.py` is a one-shot, idempotent CLI:

1. Open `../data/cards.json` and walk entries in order.
2. For each entry: mint a fresh `uuid4` for `id`; reuse the original slug-id as `slug`. If the slug has already been claimed by a previous row in the same run, append `-2/-3` exactly like the legacy code.
3. Map fields: `category` carries over; `group` defaults to `技术类` (the inline-but-dropped value the old admin form collected); `tags`/`summary`/`cover`/`title`/`url` carry over verbatim; `archived` defaults to `False` if missing.
4. For `category='local'`, read `../content/<markdown>` into `cards.body`, then call `markdown.render(body)` and store `body_html`. The old MD file is left untouched.
5. For covers under `../static/covers/`, copy to `<static_dir>/covers/<card_id>.<ext>` and rewrite `cards.cover` to the new public URL.
6. Seed the admin user from `LG_ADMIN_USERNAME`/`LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD` if no users exist (this is also done by alembic 0002, so the script is a superset for fresh dev DBs).
7. Idempotency: keyed by `slug`. If a card with that slug already exists, the row is updated (title/summary/tags/cover/body refreshed) but `id` and `created_at` are preserved. A `--dry-run` prints the diff without writing.
8. Rollback: the legacy `data/cards.json` and `content/notes/` are flipped to read-only (`chmod -R a-w`) post-migration. To roll back, drop the SQLite DB, re-flip permissions, and re-deploy the previous backend tag. Because the migration never mutates the legacy files, rollback is zero-data-loss.

## Deployment plan

Single host. nginx terminates TLS and serves both the SPA and the backend. One systemd unit runs gunicorn + uvicorn worker.

`deploy/systemd/linkgarden.service`:
```
[Unit]
Description=LinkGarden API
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=notify
User=linkgarden
Group=linkgarden
WorkingDirectory=/opt/linkgarden/backend
EnvironmentFile=/etc/linkgarden/linkgarden.env
ExecStartPre=/opt/linkgarden/backend/.venv/bin/alembic upgrade head
ExecStart=/opt/linkgarden/backend/.venv/bin/gunicorn app.asgi:app \
  -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker -w 2 \
  --bind 127.0.0.1:5001 --timeout 60 --graceful-timeout 30 \
  --access-logfile - --error-logfile -
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ReadWritePaths=/opt/linkgarden/static /opt/linkgarden/data

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

`deploy/nginx/linkgarden.conf` sketch:
```
server {
  listen 443 ssl http2;
  server_name linkgarden.example;
  root /opt/linkgarden/frontend/dist;
  index index.html;

  location /api/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5001;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
  }location /static/ {
    alias /opt/linkgarden/static/;
    expires 7d;
    add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
  }location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
  }
}
```

Rollout order on each deploy:
1. `scripts/deploy.sh` builds backend wheel via `uv build`, syncs to `/opt/linkgarden/backend`, runs `uv sync --frozen`.
2. `pnpm --dir frontend build` produces `dist/`; rsync to `/opt/linkgarden/frontend/dist`.
3. `systemctl restart linkgarden.service` — alembic runs as `ExecStartPre`; if migration fails the unit fails and nginx keeps serving the previous SPA against the previous API (because we never overwrite `dist/` until after `restart` succeeds in CI gate). Healthcheck: `curl -f http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/v1/health` before flipping the dist symlink.
4. Smoke check: `curl /api/v1/cards | jq 'length'` matches the previous count within tolerance.

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Cutover breaks deep links — old URLs are `/card/<old-slug-id>` and need to keep resolving | Migration sets `slug` to the legacy id verbatim; lookup is by slug; no URL changes for end users |
| nh3 allowlist too strict and breaks existing notes (e.g., raw `<details>`) | Audit corpus during migration: `migrate_from_json.py --dry-run --report-html` flags any node dropped by sanitizer; expand allowlist in one place if a legitimate tag is missing |
| Layered architecture adds boilerplate that slows feature work | Constrain layers to what pays for itself: services own logic, repos own SQL, routers stay ≤ 30 LOC. No DTO mappers — Pydantic does it. Keep one layer for trivial CRUD if a service body is only delegation |
| Async SQLAlchemy + SQLite contention under multiple workers | Single writer pattern: `-w 2` workers, WAL mode, `busy_timeout=5000`, `PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON` set in connect listener. Switch to Postgres via DSN swap when contention shows up — code path is identical |
| JWT secret leaks because it sits on disk | Loaded via `LG_JWT_SECRET` from `/etc/linkgarden/linkgarden.env` (mode0600, owned by `linkgarden`); rotate by changing env and `systemctl restart`; all tokens invalidate naturally |
| Cover upload accepts crafted images that exploit Pillow | Pin Pillow version, run `Image.verify()` then re-open with `Image.open` (verify destroys the parser state); reject animated formats; also enforce magic-byte sniff before Pillow even sees the bytes |
| openapi-fetch types drift from runtime when schemas change | `pnpm gen:api` regenerates `src/api/generated/schema.d.ts` from `/openapi.json`; CI fails if the file is dirty. A contract test in `backend/tests/integration/test_openapi.py` snapshots the OpenAPI doc to catch silent breakages |
| Legacy `/api/*` shim hides callers that never updated | The308 handler logs a `legacy_api_hit` warning with the original path and User-Agent; release notes promise removal one minor later |
| md-editor-v3 ships its own highlight.js copy and bloats the bundle | pnpm `overrides.highlight.js` pins a single version; Vite manualChunks splits `highlight.js` into its own chunk so detail page and editor share it |
| Admin password seeded via env shows up in shell history | `seed_admin.py` reads from `getpass()` when stdin is a TTY; alembic 0002 only runs if `LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD` is non-empty and inserts via parameterized SQL; rotation script available |
| pnpm + Vite 7 require Node 20.19+ — wrong Node breaks CI | `package.json` `engines` + `.nvmrc`; CI matrix pins Node 22LTS; deploy script verifies `node --version` before build |
| 308 redirect on legacy POSTs strips the body in some clients | Document explicitly; 308 preserves method+body per RFC 7538, but if a known client misbehaves, switch the legacy path to a server-side rewrite that calls the v1 handler directly |

## Overview
LinkGarden is reorganized as a feature-modular monorepo: every domain concept (cards, auth, covers, admin, tags) gets one folder on each side of the stack with its routes, services, repositories, schemas, store, views, and components living together. The backend is a FastAPI `src/app` layout where each feature owns a router that the app composes at `/api/v1`, persisting through SQLAlchemy 2.0 async repositories on aiosqlite (dev) / asyncpg (prod). Card identity becomes a UUID primary key plus a regenerable unique slug, markdown is rendered and sanitized server-side, and a real `User` table backs JWT auth. The frontend mirrors the same feature split under `src/features/*`, talks to the backend through openapi-typescript + openapi-fetch (zero axios), and renders pre-sanitized HTML with `v-html` while highlight.js paints code blocks. Deployment is one systemd unit (gunicorn + UvicornWorker) fronted by nginx, with `alembic upgrade head` as the unit's `ExecStartPre`; the frontend is `rsync`ed as a static `dist/` bundle.

### SQLAlchemy models

#### `users`
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `Mapped[UUID]` | PK, server default `gen_random_uuid()` on pg / app-side `uuid4()` on sqlite |
| `username` | `Mapped[str]` | unique, citext-ish via lower-index on sqlite |
| `password_hash` | `Mapped[str]` | bcrypt |
| `role` | `Mapped[str]` | enum-ish: `admin` (only role today) |
| `created_at` | `Mapped[datetime]` | UTC, server default now |
| `updated_at` | `Mapped[datetime]` | UTC, on update now |

Indexes: `ix_users_username_lower` (unique, functional `lower(username)`).
Relationships: none for now (cards are global, not per-user).

#### `cards`
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `Mapped[UUID]` | PK, immutable |
| `slug` | `Mapped[str]` | unique among non-archived; partial index |
| `title` | `Mapped[str]` | non-empty, length-bound 1..200 |
| `category` | `Mapped[str]` | enum `external` \| `local` |
| `group` | `Mapped[str]` | content group enum `技术类` \| `随笔类` \| `生活类`; extensible (CHECK not enforced at DB; validated in schema) |
| `summary` | `Mapped[str]` | default `""` |
| `cover` | `Mapped[str \| None]` | public URL (`/covers/<id>.<ext>` or external) |
| `url` | `Mapped[str \| None]` | required when `category=external` |
| `body` | `Mapped[str \| None]` | raw markdown when `category=local` |
| `archived` | `Mapped[bool]` | default `False`, not null |
| `created_at` | `Mapped[datetime]` | UTC, server default now |
| `updated_at` | `Mapped[datetime]` | UTC, on update now |

Indexes:
- `ix_cards_slug_active` partial unique on `(slug) WHERE archived = FALSE`
- `ix_cards_created_at` desc
- `ix_cards_category`
- `ix_cards_group`

#### `card_tags`
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `card_id` | `Mapped[UUID]` | FK -> `cards.id` ON DELETE CASCADE |
| `tag` | `Mapped[str]` | trimmed, lower-bounded 1..40|

Composite PK `(card_id, tag)`. Index `ix_card_tags_tag`. Relationship `Card.tags: list[str]` exposed via `association_proxy` for clean Pydantic mapping.

### Pydantic schemas

Located in each feature's `schemas.py`. All use `ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)`.

`features/cards/schemas.py`:
- `CardBase` — title, category, group, summary, cover, tags
- `CardCreate(CardBase)` — adds `url?`, `content?`, optional `slug?`; cross-field `@model_validator` enforces (category=external ⇒ url) and (category=local ⇒ content)
- `CardUpdate` — every field `Optional`; same cross-field rule when fields supplied
- `ArchivePatch` — `archived: bool` (no default; client must send it)
- `CardRead` — public list/detail shape: id, slug, title, category, group, summary, tags, cover, url?, created_at, updated_at, archived
- `CardDetail(CardRead)` — adds `content: str | None` (raw md, local only) and `content_html: str | None` (rendered+sanitized)
- `CardFilters` — `category?`, `group?`, `tag?`, `q?`, `include_archived: bool = False` (parsed via `Depends` as query params)

`features/auth/schemas.py`:
- `LoginIn` — `username`, `password`
- `TokenOut` — `access_token`, `token_type="bearer"`, `expires_in`
- `UserRead` — `id`, `username`, `role`, `created_at`

`features/covers/schemas.py`:
- `CoverUploadOut` — `url`, `width`, `height`, `bytes`

`core/errors.py`:
- `ErrorEnvelope` — `ok: Literal[False] = False`, `error: str`, `code: str`

### API v1 contract

All paths prefixed `/api/v1`. Auth column: `none` (public), `bearer` (admin JWT). Legacy `/api/*` paths return 308 to the corresponding `/api/v1/*` for one release.

| Method | Path | Auth | Request | 2xx | 4xx | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GET | `/health` | none | – | `200{"ok": true}` | – | Liveness |
| POST | `/auth/login` | none | `LoginIn` | `200 TokenOut` | `401 invalid_credentials` | HS256 JWT, 12h TTL |
| GET | `/auth/me` | bearer | – | `200 UserRead` | `401 unauthorized` | Identity probe |
| GET | `/cards` | none | query: `category? group? tag? q? include_archived?` | `200 list[CardRead]` | `422 validation_error` | `q` matches title+summary+tags ci; archived excluded by default |
| GET | `/cards/{slug}` | none | path slug | `200 CardDetail` | `404 not_found` | Local cards include `content` + `content_html` |
| POST | `/cards` | bearer | `CardCreate` | `201 CardDetail` | `400 invalid_payload`, `409 slug_conflict`, `422 validation_error` | Mints UUID, derives slug from title/explicit slug; collision suffix `-2`, `-3`... |
| PUT | `/cards/{slug}` | bearer | `CardUpdate` | `200 CardDetail` | `404 not_found`, `422 validation_error`, `400 invalid_payload` | Partial; can switch category; slug is regenerated only if `slug` field present |
| PATCH | `/cards/{slug}/archive` | bearer | `ArchivePatch` | `200 CardRead` | `404 not_found`, `422 validation_error` | Setter (no default; surprise-free) |
| DELETE | `/cards/{slug}` | bearer | – | `204` | `404 not_found` | Hard delete (UI keeps it hidden by default) |
| GET | `/tags` | none | – | `200 list[str]` | – | Distinct, sorted, non-archived only |
| POST | `/covers` | bearer | multipart `file`, `card_id` | `201 CoverUploadOut` | `400 invalid_image`, `404 card_not_found`, `413 payload_too_large` | Writes `<static>/covers/<card_id>.<ext>`; updates `cards.cover` |
| GET | `/admin/status` | bearer | – | `200 {bootstrapped, version}` | `401` | Diagnostics endpoint |

### Auth flow

1. Admin POSTs `/api/v1/auth/login` with `{username, password}`. `features/auth/service.authenticate()` loads the user, `bcrypt.checkpw`s the hash, and on success returns a signed JWT (`HS256`, secret from `LG_JWT_SECRET`, claims `sub=<user.id>`, `role`, `iat`, `exp` = now + `LG_JWT_TTL_MINUTES`).
2. Client stores the token in the Pinia `useSessionStore` (in-memory, mirrored to `localStorage` only for survival across reloads) and attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every mutation through an `openapi-fetch` middleware.
3. `features/auth/deps.current_user` extracts the header, decodes the JWT pinned to `algorithms=["HS256"]` (never accept `none`), loads the user, and raises `AppError("unauthorized", 401)` on any failure. `require_admin` adds a role check (today: always admin).
4. Initial admin is seeded by Alembic data migration `0002_seed_admin.py`, which reads `LG_ADMIN_USERNAME` and `LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD` from env, hashes once, and inserts if absent (idempotent).

### Markdown pipeline

`services/markdown.py` exposes a single pure function:

```
render_markdown(md: str) -> str
```

Steps:
1. Strip a single leading `# H1` line (regex `^# .*\n+`).
2. Feed remaining text into `markdown-it-py` (`MarkdownIt("commonmark", {"linkify": True, "html": False, "breaks": False})`) with plugins: `mdit_py_plugins.footnote`, `tables` (built-in via gfm-like preset), `fence` (built-in), `linkify`.
3. Walk the token stream to attach `data-language="<lang>"` to fence tokens; emit `<pre><code class="hljs language-<lang>" data-language="<lang>">`.
4. Sanitize the HTML with `nh3.clean(html, tags=ALLOWED_TAGS, attributes=ALLOWED_ATTRS, link_rel="noopener nofollow")`. Allowlist: `p, h1..h6, blockquote, ul, ol, li, code, pre, em, strong, del, hr, br, a (href,title,rel,target), img (src,alt,title,loading), table, thead, tbody, tr, th, td`; `code/pre` keep `class` (`hljs language-*`) and `data-language`.
5. Return the sanitized HTML. Output is cached with `functools.lru_cache(maxsize=512)` keyed on `(md, version)`; the cache is invalidated when the row is updated by mixing `cards.updated_at` into the key inside `service.py`.

Frontend renders the resulting string via `v-html` and runs a client-side highlight.js pass that reads `data-language`. No DOMPurify is shipped.

### Cover upload pipeline

`POST /api/v1/covers` consumes `multipart/form-data`:
- `file` — image binary
- `card_id` — UUID of the target card (must exist; admin only)

`features/covers/service.upload_cover(file, card)`:
1. Reject content-type not in `{image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp}` → `400 invalid_image_type`.
2. Stream into a `SpooledTemporaryFile`; if total > 4 MiB → `413 payload_too_large`.
3. Open with `Pillow.Image.open(...)`, `verify()` then re-open for real metadata; reject dimensions outside `64..2400 px` either side → `400 invalid_image_dimensions`.
4. Compute extension from MIME (`png|jpg|webp`); target path `<settings.static_dir>/covers/<card_id>.<ext>`.
5. Write atomically: write to `<target>.tmp` then `os.replace` to final path. Delete any previously stored cover with a different extension for the same card_id.
6. Update `cards.cover = "/covers/<card_id>.<ext>"` in the same transaction.
7. Return `CoverUploadOut{url, width, height, bytes}`.

Static files are served by FastAPI in dev (`app.mount("/covers", StaticFiles(directory=settings.static_dir / "covers"))`) and by nginx `alias` in prod.

### Error envelope and exception handlers

Success: the resource (or `204` no body).
Failure: `{"ok": false, "error": "<human>", "code": "<machine>"}` with proper HTTP status.

In `core/errors.py`:
- `class AppError(Exception)` carries `code`, `message`, `status_code`.
- `register_error_handlers(app)` installs:
  - `AppError` → `JSONResponse(status=err.status_code, content=ErrorEnvelope(error=err.message, code=err.code).model_dump())`.
  - `HTTPException` → same envelope, code derived from `detail.code` if structured, else slug of `phrase(status_code)` (e.g. `not_found`, `unauthorized`).
  - `RequestValidationError` → `422` with code `validation_error` and `error` summarizing the first failure (`<loc>: <msg>`).
  - `Exception` (catchall) → `500 internal_error`, logged at ERROR with `structlog.contextvars`.

`/api/v1/health` bypasses the envelope and returns `{"ok": true}` per the locked decision.

### Pinia stores

All written as setup stores (Pinia 3, ESM only). `$reset` is hand-rolled where needed.

| Store | State | Actions |
|---|---|---|
| `useSessionStore` (`stores/session.ts`) | `token: string \| null`, `user: UserRead \| null`, `isAdmin = computed(...)` | `login(creds)`, `logout()`, `restore()` (read from `localStorage` on boot), `setToken(t)` (persists to `localStorage`) |
| `useCardsStore` (`features/cards/store.ts`) | `list: CardRead[]`, `byId: Map<string, CardDetail>`, `filters: CardFilters`, `loading`, `error` | `fetchList()`, `fetchDetail(slug)`, `publish(payload)`, `update(slug, patch)`, `archive(slug, val)`, `remove(slug)`, `setFilter(partial)`, `$reset()` |
| `useTagsStore` (`features/tags/store.ts`) | `tags: string[]`, `loading` | `fetch()` |
| `useToastStore` (`shared/composables/useToast.ts` exports a store) | `queue: Toast[]` | `push(t)`, `dismiss(id)` |

### Routes and guards

`router/index.ts` calls `createRouter({ history: createWebHistory() })` and registers per-feature route arrays. A global `beforeEach` guard reads `useSessionStore` and redirects unauth admin hits to `/login?next=...`.

| Path | Name | Component | Meta | Guard |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `home` | `features/cards/views/HomeView.vue` | `{ chrome: "public" }` | – |
| `/card/:slug` | `card-detail` | `features/cards/views/CardDetailView.vue` | `{ chrome: "public" }` | resolves slug;404 view on error |
| `/login` | `login` | `features/auth/views/LoginView.vue` | `{ chrome: "minimal" }` | redirect to `next` if already logged in |
| `/admin` | `admin-cards` | `features/cards/views/AdminCardsView.vue` (inside `AdminShellView`) | `{ chrome: "admin", requiresAdmin: true }` | global guard checks `isAdmin` |
| `/admin/publish` | `admin-publish` | `features/cards/views/AdminPublishView.vue` | `{ chrome: "admin", requiresAdmin: true }` | global guard |
| `/admin/publish/:slug` | `admin-edit` | `features/cards/views/AdminPublishView.vue` | `{ chrome: "admin", requiresAdmin: true }` | guard + pre-load |
| `/:pathMatch(.*)*` | `not-found` | `shared/ui/NotFoundView.vue` | `{ chrome: "public" }` | – |

### Composables and shared utils

- `shared/api/client.ts` — exports `api` = `createClient<paths>({ baseUrl: "/api/v1" })` with a `middleware` that injects `Authorization: Bearer ${useSessionStore().token}` when present, and normalizes non-2xx into `AppError` via `mapError`.
- `shared/api/errors.ts` — `class AppError extends Error { code: string; status: number }`; `mapError(envelope)` reads `{ok:false,error,code}` and returns the typed error.
- `shared/composables/useAsync.ts` — `useAsync(fn)` returns `{ data, error, loading, run }` for one-shot calls without pulling in vue-query.
- `shared/composables/useDebounce.ts` — for the search input.
- `shared/composables/useToast.ts` — global toaster.
- `shared/utils/slug.ts` — client-side display slug preview for the publish form (server is source of truth).
- `shared/utils/format.ts` — date + byte formatting.
- Codegen script `scripts/gen-api.sh`: `pnpm openapi-typescript http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/v1/openapi.json -o src/shared/api/schema.d.ts`. Run via `pnpm gen:api`.

- `features/cards/components/ArticleBody.vue` — accepts `html: string`, renders via `v-html`, after each update walks `pre code[data-language]` and applies highlight.js. No DOMPurify (server pre-sanitized).

- `features/cards/components/CardFilters.vue` — bound to `useCardsStore.filters`; emits debounced changes; renders category sidebar that actually drives the filter.
- `features/cards/components/PublishForm.vue` — wraps `md-editor-v3`; bottom 附加信息 region holds summary/tags/cover/group; on submit picks `publish` vs `update` based on route.

## Migration plan

`backend/scripts/migrate_from_json.py` is the one-shot importer; rerunning it is a no-op.

Behavior:
1. Open `DATABASE_URL` via the same async session factory. Run inside a single transaction per card.
2. Read `../data/cards.json` (kept frozen for rollback).
3. For each legacy card:
   - `legacy_id = card["id"]` becomes `slug`.
   - Mint a fresh `uuid4()` as the row `id`.
   - Map fields: `title`, `category`, `summary`, `cover`, `url`, `archived` (default `False`), `tags`.
   - `group`: take `card.get("group")` if present, else default `"技术类"`; logged for manual review.
   - `created_at`: parse `YYYY-MM-DD`, attach UTC tz; `updated_at = created_at`.
   - For `category=local`: read `../content/notes/<slug>.md`; store full text in `cards.body`.
4. Idempotency key: `slug`. If a row with that slug already exists, skip (no overwrite, no duplicate). The script logs `created | skipped` per row.
5. Cover files in legacy `data/cards.json` that already point to `/covers/<id>.png` are validated; missing files are warned but not fatal.
6. Admin seed: also creates the admin user from `LG_ADMIN_USERNAME` / `LG_ADMIN_PASSWORD` if absent (same logic as Alembic data migration; safe to call twice).

Rollback:
- The legacy `data/cards.json` and `content/notes/*.md` are kept read-only as a snapshot. To roll back, stop the new unit, restore the old Flask `app.py` from git, point nginx back to the old upstream. Data hasn't been mutated in the legacy tree.
- The DB itself can be dropped (`alembic downgrade base`) without touching the snapshot.
- The frontend legacy bundle is kept as a `dist-prev/` tarball on the host; nginx `root` swap is a one-liner.

## Deployment plan

Single host, single nginx, one systemd unit.

`deploy/systemd/linkgarden-backend.service`:

```
[Unit]
Description=LinkGarden backend (gunicorn + UvicornWorker)
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=notify
User=linkgarden
Group=linkgarden
WorkingDirectory=/srv/linkgarden/backend
EnvironmentFile=/etc/linkgarden/backend.env
ExecStartPre=/srv/linkgarden/venv/bin/alembic -c alembic.ini upgrade head
ExecStart=/srv/linkgarden/venv/bin/gunicorn app.main:app \-k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker \
    -w 2 --bind 127.0.0.1:5001 \
    --access-logfile - --error-logfile - \
    --timeout 60 --graceful-timeout 30
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ReadWritePaths=/srv/linkgarden/backend/src/app/static /srv/linkgarden/var

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

`deploy/nginx/linkgarden.conf` sketch:

```
server {
  listen 443 ssl http2;
  server_name linkgarden.example;

  root /srv/linkgarden/frontend/dist;
  index index.html;

  location /api/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5001;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
  }

  location /covers/ {
    alias /srv/linkgarden/backend/src/app/static/covers/;
    expires 7d;
    access_log off;
  }

  location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
  }
}
```

Alembic step: `ExecStartPre` runs `alembic upgrade head` against `LG_DATABASE_URL` before gunicorn binds. Failures abort the unit (systemd `Restart=on-failure`); the previous binary keeps running because the swap is atomic.

Rollout order:
1. `pnpm --filter frontend build` locally; verify dist size and openapi codegen freshness.
2. `rsync` `backend/` tree → `/srv/linkgarden/backend/`; install/refresh venv with `uv sync --frozen`.
3. `systemctl restart linkgarden-backend` (triggers `alembic upgrade head` then gunicorn).
4. `curl -fsS https://linkgarden.example/api/v1/health` smoke.
5. `rsync` `frontend/dist/` → `/srv/linkgarden/frontend/dist/`.
6. `nginx -t && systemctl reload nginx`.
7. Smoke: home, card detail (local), card detail (external), login, publish, archive, cover upload.

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| URL break: `/card/<old-id>` is now `/card/<slug>`, but legacy IDs already are slugs, so most URLs hold — minor variance possible | Migration mints `slug = legacy_id`; add a 404 fallback view that queries `GET /cards?q=<slug>` and offers a redirect link |
| `alembic upgrade head` fails on deploy (e.g., bad migration) | `ExecStartPre` aborts startup; previous unit keeps serving until manual fix; CI runs `alembic upgrade head` against an ephemeral sqlite to catch issues pre-merge |
| Server-side sanitizer (nh3) removes legitimate authoring constructs | Allowlist documented in `services/markdown.py`; integration tests cover code/tables/links/images/footnotes; `markdown.py` exposes a CLI to render a single file for visual diffing |
| `expire_on_commit=False` plus stale ORM objects after commit cause subtle bugs | Service layer always returns the result of an explicit re-`SELECT` after write, not the mutated ORM instance |
| JWT in `localStorage` is XSS-readable | Server pre-sanitizes markdown, so v-html input is trusted; CSP `script-src 'self'` set in nginx; no third-party widgets; TTL kept short (12h) and renewable on `/auth/me` |
| Cover upload abused to fill disk | `LG_MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES` (4 MiB), per-extension validation, one cover per card overwrites previous file, monitoring on `static/covers/` directory size |
| `category`↔ `group` confusion (storage type vs content group) | Two columns, separate enums, both round-tripped in `CardRead` and form UI; tests assert the publish form sends `group` (legacy bug) |
| `cards.json` read-modify-write race during cutover | New backend never writes the legacy file; the importer reads it once; legacy tree is set chmod 444 immediately after migration |
| Highlight.js double-wrap | `CodeBlock.vue` sets `data-highlighted` and short-circuits; `ArticleBody.vue` selects `pre code[data-language]:not([data-highlighted])` |
| Vite 7 / Node version drift between dev and CI | `engines.node = ">=20.19.0"` in `package.json`, `.nvmrc`, CI uses the same image as the Docker build |
| openapi-typescript output drift after backend change | `pnpm gen:api` is part of the pre-commit hook; CI fails if `git diff --exit-code src/shared/api/schema.d.ts` shows changes |
